column1: Independent Commissioner Against Corruption and Office for Public Integrity
Section 31(1) Police Complaints and Discipline Act 2016
Complaints and reports received under the PCD Act
1 Under the PCD Act the OPI has oversight of matters relating to Protective Security Officers.
Method of receipt of complaints and reports received under the PCD Act
Rounding has been used in respect to statistical results. Accordingly not all tables and figures total 100%.
General nature of complaints and reports received under the ICAC Act 1
Conduct in private capacity, failure to disclose/declare to employer
Failure of duty, inaction or inadequate exercise of power
Improper budgeting, procurement, contract management, accounts payable
Improper or unauthorised use of employment entitlements
Improper use, damage or theft of resources
Improper use/access of information and improper record keeping
Improper, unauthorised or mismanaged exercise of power
Other criminal conduct while acting in capacity as a public officer

1 On 1 July 2020 the OPI introduced a new ‘general nature’ classification scheme replacing the previous categories which were devised prior to the commencement of ICAC/OPI in September 2013, and had been in use since that date. The reformed scheme was devised and implemented in response to several reviews of the use, accuracy and reliability of internal complaints and reports data, which found the previous scheme was no longer fit for purpose. The efficacy of these categories is relied upon for internal research, trend and risk analysis, strategic planning, and corruption prevention.
Rounding has been used in respect of statistical results. Accordingly not all tables and figures total 100%.
General nature complaints and reports received under the PCD Act
Alleged offence while not acting in capacity as public officer
Conduct (other than criminal conduct) of a public officer while not acting in capacity as a public officer
Conflict of interest - tender assessment / procurement / contract / vote / decision making
Deception associated with employment / appointment to public office / coercion
Failure to exercise power / make decision / carry out a function or inadequate exercise of power / inappropriate exercise of power / function
Inappropriate conduct / workplace bullying / harassment / failure to comply with policy / procedure
Mismanagement in relation to the performance of official duties
Misuse of public money / theft from the workplace
Other criminal conduct while acting in capacity
1 Figures do not include six matters relating to Protective Security Officers.
Rounding has been used in respect to statistical results. Accordingly not all tables and figures total 100%.
